{{Short description|Film award ceremony in 2025}} {{Use American English|date=January 2025}} {{Use mdy dates|date=January 2025}} {{Infobox film awards | number = 82 | award = Golden Globe Awards | image = 82nd Golden Globe Awards.png | caption = Official poster | date = January 5, 2025 | site = [[The Beverly Hilton]],
[[Beverly Hills, California]], U.S. | host = [[Nikki Glaser]] | director = [[Glenn Weiss]] | best_film_drama = ''[[The Brutalist]]'' | best_film_musical_or_comedy = ''[[Emilia Pérez]]'' | best_drama_series = ''[[Shōgun (2024 TV series)|Shōgun]]'' | best_musical_or_comedy_series = ''[[Hacks (TV series)|Hacks]]'' | best_miniseries_or_tv_movie = ''[[Baby Reindeer]]'' | most_wins = Film: ''[[Emilia Pérez]]'' (4)
TV: ''[[Shōgun (2024 TV series)|Shōgun]]'' (4) | most_nominations = {{nowrap|Film: ''[[Emilia Pérez]]'' (10)}}
TV: ''[[The Bear (TV series)|The Bear]]'' (5) | network = [[CBS]]
[[Paramount+]] (streaming) | ratings = {{nowrap|10.1 million ([[VideoAmp]])
9.3 million ([[Nielsen Media Research#Nielsen TV ratings|Nielsen ratings]]){{cite magazine |url=https://variety.com/2025/tv/news/golden-globes-2025-ratings-1236267097/ |title=Golden Globes 2025 Ratings: 9.3 Million Viewers, Down 2% from Last Year |last=Hailu |first=Selome |magazine=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=January 6, 2025 |access-date=January 22, 2025 |quote=Though [[Paramount Global|Paramount]] initially reported that 10.1 million average viewers tuned into the Golden Globes using data from VideoAmp — which would have marked a 7% increase from last year's 9.4 million — industry standard measurement company [[Nielsen Media Research|Nielsen]] counted a 2% decrease to 9.3 million viewers.}}}} | last = [[81st Golden Globe Awards|81st]] | next = [[83rd Golden Globe Awards|83rd]] }} The '''82nd Golden Globe Awards''' was an awards ceremony that honored [[2024 in film|excellence in film]] and [[2024 in American television|American television productions of 2024]]. The winners were revealed during the live telecast, airing on [[CBS]] and streaming on [[Paramount+]] on January 5, 2025.{{cite web |last=Lowry |first=Brian |date=January 5, 2025 |title='The Brutalist' and 'Emilia Pérez' emerge with top Golden Globes prizes |url=https://goldenglobes.com/articles/the-brutalist-and-emilia-perez-emerge-with-top-golden-globes-prizes/ |access-date=January 5, 2025 |publisher=[[Golden Globe Awards|Golden Globes]] |quote=''[[The Brutalist]]'' and ''[[Emilia Pérez]]'' took the top movie prizes at the 82nd annual Golden Globe Awards, emerging as the only two films to garner multiple wins on a night that saw a mix of newcomers and familiar faces walk away with hardware at the venerable ceremony.}} The event was hosted by comedian [[Nikki Glaser]], making her the first solo female host in Golden Globes history.{{cite magazine |date=August 28, 2024 |title=Nikki Glaser Named 2025 Golden Globe Host—First Host Since Jo Koy's Criticized Performance |magazine=[[Forbes]] |last=Murray |first=Conor |url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/conormurray/2024/08/28/nikki-glaser-named-2025-golden-globe-host-first-host-since-jo-koys-criticized-performance/ |access-date=December 10, 2024 |editor-last=Lane |editor-first=Randall |editor-link=Randall Lane (journalist) |url-access=limited |issn=0015-6914 |oclc=6465733 |quote=[[Nikki Glaser|Glaser]], a comedian known for her celebrity roasts and stand-up specials, said hosting the Golden Globes is a 'dream job'...}} The nominations were announced live on December 9, 2024, at a [[press conference]] at [[the Beverly Hilton]] by actors [[Morris Chestnut]] and [[Mindy Kaling]], following an introduction from Golden Globes president, Helen Hoenhe.{{cite web |title=Nominations Announced for 82nd Annual Golden Globes |url=https://goldenglobes.com/articles/nominations-announced-for-82nd-annual-golden-globes/ |date=December 9, 2024 |access-date=December 10, 2024 |publisher=[[Golden Globe Awards|Golden Globes]] |quote=[[Morris Chestnut]] and [[Mindy Kaling]] flank Golden Globes president Helen Hoenhe at the Dec. 9, 2024, nominations announcement.}} ''[[Emilia Pérez]]'', with 10 nominations, became the most nominated comedy/musical film in [[Golden Globe Awards#Superlatives|Golden Globes history]], just 1 shy of the overall record of 11, set by [[Robert Altman]]'s [[country music]] [[satire (film and television)|satire]] ''[[Nashville (film)|Nashville]]'' in the drama category in [[33rd Golden Globe Awards|1976]].{{cite magazine |date=December 9, 2024 |title=''Emilia Pérez'' Sets Golden Globes Record with 10 Nominations |magazine=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |last=Couch |first=Aaron |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/2025-golden-globes-emilia-perez-nominations-1236081326/ |access-date=December 10, 2024 |editor-last=Moody |editor-first=Nekesa Mumbi |editor-link=Nekesa Mumbi Moody |issn=0018-3660 |oclc=44653726 |quote=''[[Emilia Pérez]]'' had a record-breaking morning, with the [[Netflix]] film landing ten Golden Globe nominations, making it the most nominated comedy or musical film in the awards show's history.}} ''[[The Brutalist]]'' received the second-most in film with 7 nominations, followed by ''[[Conclave (film)|Conclave]]'' with 6.{{cite magazine |date=December 9, 2024 |title=''Emilia Pérez'' Picks Up Record-Breaking 10 Golden Globe Nominations |magazine=[[Rolling Stone]] |last=Richardson |first=Kalia |url=https://www.rollingstone.com/tv-movies/tv-movie-news/emilia-perez-record-10-golden-globe-nominations-1235197324/ |access-date=December 9, 2024 |editor-last=Woods |editor-first=Sean |url-access=limited |issn=0035-791X |oclc=969027590 |quote=In the drama category, [[Brady Corbet]]'s historical drama ''[[The Brutalist]]'' scooped up seven nominations...}} Additionally, ''[[The Bear (TV series)|The Bear]]'' led the television nominations with 5, followed by ''[[Only Murders in the Building]]'' and ''[[Shōgun (2024 TV series)|Shōgun]]'' with 4 each.{{cite magazine |date=December 9, 2024 |title=Golden Globes 2025 Nominations: ''Emilia Pérez'' Dominates Films with 10 Nods; ''The Bear'' Leads TV with Five |magazine=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |last1=Donnelly |first1=Matt |url=https://variety.com/2024/film/awards/golden-globes-nominations-2025-full-list-1236236911/ |access-date=December 9, 2024 |last2=Moreau |first2=Jordan |issn=0042-2738 |oclc=60626328 |quote=On the TV side, [[FX (TV channel)|FX]]/[[Hulu]]'s ''[[The Bear (TV series)|The Bear]]'' continues its trophy domination with the most nominations for any series; that's five for the restaurant-set [[Comedy drama|dramedy]]...}} ==Ceremony information== On August 28, 2024, comedian [[Nikki Glaser]] was announced as the host for this year's ceremony, following last year's widely criticized host, comedian [[Jo Koy]]. She is the first woman to host the Golden Globes solo.{{cite web |date=December 18, 2024 |title=Nikki Glaser on making history as 1st woman to host Golden Globes solo |url=https://www.yahoo.com/news/nikki-glaser-making-history-1st-125857679.html |access-date=January 5, 2025 |publisher=[[Yahoo News]]}} Glaser herself was nominated for [[Golden Globe Award for Best Performance in Stand-Up Comedy on Television|Best Performance in Stand-Up Comedy on Television]] for her [[HBO]] comedy special, ''Nikki Glaser: Someday You'll Die'',{{cite web |title=Golden Globes 2025: ''Emilia Pérez'' scores 10 nominations as Kate Winslet, Selena Gomez and Sebastian Stan each take two |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2024/dec/09/golden-globes-2025-emilia-perez-scores-10-nominations-as-kate-winslet-and-sebastian-stan-each-take-two |last=Shoard |first=Catherine |date=December 9, 2024 |access-date=December 11, 2024 |website=[[The Guardian]] |editor-last=Viner |editor-first=Katharine |editor-link=Katharine Viner |issn=0261-3077 |oclc=60623878 |eissn=1756-3224 |quote=They will also host a standalone ceremony for the recipients of their lifetime achievement awards, with video highlights of [[Viola Davis]] and [[Ted Danson]] picking up their [[Golden Globe Cecil B. DeMille Award|Cecil B. DeMille Award]] and [[Golden Globe Carol Burnett Award|Carol Burnett Award]] honours then played during the main ceremony.}} but lost to [[Netflix]]'s ''Ali Wong: Single Lady''.{{cite web |last=Tinoco |first=Armando |date=January 5, 2025 |title=Nikki Glaser Roasts Herself as 'First-Time Golden Globe Loser' After Ali Wong Award Win |url=https://deadline.com/2025/01/nikki-glaser-roasts-herself-golden-globes-1236247623/ |access-date=January 6, 2025 |website=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |quote=[[Nikki Glaser|Glaser]] lost to [[Ali Wong]] and her [[Netflix]] special ''Single Lady''.}} The ceremony was held at 5:00 p.m. [[Pacific Time Zone|PST]] / 8:00 p.m. [[Eastern Time Zone|EST]] on January 5, 2025. It was broadcast live on [[CBS]] and streamed on [[Paramount+]]. [[Glenn Weiss]] and [[Ricky Kirshner]] of White Cherry Entertainment returned from last year to executive produce the ceremony, along with [[Dick Clark Productions]]. On December 3, 2024, it was announced that the two Lifetime Achievement Awards, which were not presented last year, would return this year; however, they were presented during an untelevised gala dinner on January 3, 2025, prior to the telecast, similar to the [[Academy Awards]]' [[Governors Awards]]. The recipient of the [[Golden Globe Cecil B. DeMille Award|Cecil B. DeMille Award]] was [[Viola Davis]], while the [[Golden Globe Carol Burnett Award|Carol Burnett Award]] was awarded to [[Ted Danson]].{{cite web |title=Viola Davis and Ted Danson to Receive Top Honors at the 2025 Golden Globes |url=https://www.indiewire.com/awards/results/viola-davis-ted-danson-golden-globes-2025-1235067713/ |last=Jones |first=Marcus |date=December 2, 2024 |access-date=December 10, 2024 |website=[[IndieWire]] |editor-last=Harris-Bridson |editor-first=Dana |quote=The pair, who have 20 Golden Globes nominations and four wins between them, will be feted at a gala dinner on January 3 before receiving their awards on the January 5 telecast.}} ==Winners and nominees== [[File:Adrien Brody-61584.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Adrien Brody]], Best Actor in a Motion Picture – Drama winner]] [[File:Fernanda Torres-68482.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Fernanda Torres]], Best Actress in a Motion Picture – Drama winner]] [[File:Sebastian Stan in 2024.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Sebastian Stan]], Best Actor in a Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy winner]] [[File:Demi Moore at the 2024 Toronto International Film Festival 3 (cropped) (cropped).jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Demi Moore]], Best Actress in a Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy winner]] [[File:Kieran Culkin at the 2024 New York Film Festival 2 (cropped II).jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Kieran Culkin]], Best Supporting Actor winner]] [[File:Zoe Saldaña at the 2024 Toronto International Film Festival (cropped).jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Zoe Saldaña]], Best Supporting Actress winner]] [[File:Hiroyuki Sanada 20240220.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Hiroyuki Sanada]], Best Actor in a Television Series – Drama winner]] [[File:Anna Sawai from Sidewalks Entertainment 2024 (cropped).jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Anna Sawai]], Best Actress in a Television Series – Drama winner]] [[File:Jeremy Allen White Springsteen-33 (cropped).jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Jeremy Allen White]], Best Actor in a Television Series – Musical or Comedy winner]] [[File:Jean Smart by Gage Skidmore.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Jean Smart]], Best Actress in a Television Series – Musical or Comedy winner]] [[File:BansheesBFI131022 (21 of 22) (52447275762) (cropped).jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Colin Farrell]], Best Actor in a Limited Series, Anthology Series, or Motion Picture Made for Television winner]] [[File:Jodie Foster-8667.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Jodie Foster]], Best Actress in a Limited Series, Anthology Series, or Motion Picture Made for Television winner]] [[File:Asano Tadanobu from "Ravens" at Red Carpet of the Tokyo International Film Festival 2024 (54577962659).jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Tadanobu Asano]], Best Supporting Actor in a Series, Limited Series, Anthology Series, or Motion Picture Made for Television winner]] [[File:Jessica Gunning at the 2025 Toronto International Film Festival (cropped).jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Jessica Gunning]], Best Supporting Actress in a Series, Limited Series, Anthology Series, or Motion Picture Made for Television winner]] ''[[Emilia Pérez]]'' star [[Karla Sofía Gascón]] was the first [[Transgender|trans]] nominee for a Golden Globe motion picture acting category, for the [[Golden Globe Award for Best Actress in a Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy|Best Actress in a Musical or Comedy]] category.{{cite magazine |last=Cooper |first=Alex |date=December 9, 2024 |editor-last=Guerrero |editor-first=Desiree |editor2-last=Broverman |editor2-first=Neal |title=Karla Sofía Gascón makes trans history with Golden Globe nomination |url=https://www.advocate.com/arts-entertainment/karla-sofia-gascon-golden-globes |access-date=December 9, 2024 |magazine=[[The Advocate (magazine)|The Advocate]] |issn=0001-8996 |quote=[[Karla Sofía Gascón]]'s performance in ''[[Emilia Pérez]]'' has made her a global sensation. Now, she's made history as the first out [[Transgender|trans]] woman to be nominated for a lead actress in a film at the Golden Globes.}} [[Jacques Audiard]], the director and writer of the film, was one of the most nominated individuals, with a total of three nominations: [[Golden Globe Award for Best Director|Best Director]], [[Golden Globe Award for Best Screenplay|Best Screenplay]], and co-lyricist of [[Golden Globe Award for Best Original Song|Best Original Song]] nominee "[[El Mal]]". The composers and co-lyricists of the song, [[Camille (French singer)|Camille]] and [[Clément Ducol]], tied Audiard for most nominated individuals. Aside from "El Mal", they were also jointly nominated for composing ''Emilia Pérez''{{'}}s [[Golden Globe Award for Best Original Score|Best Original Score]] and an additional Best Original Song nominee: "[[Mi camino]]". This year's nominations were tabulated by [[KPMG]], the U.S. audit, tax, and advisory firm, taking over [[Ernst & Young]] after fifty plus years.{{cite web |last=Gray |first=Tim |date=December 11, 2023 |title=Golden Globe Nominations Led by Barbenheimer |url=https://goldenglobes.com/articles/golden-globe-nominations-led-by-barbenheimer/ |access-date=January 30, 2026 |publisher=[[Golden Globe Awards|Golden Globes]]}}{{cite web |last=Nepales |first=Ruben V. |date=January 2, 2022 |title='And the Golden Globe Goes To...' – A Conversation with E&Y's Andy Sale |url=https://goldenglobes.com/articles/and-the-golden-globe-goes-to-a-conversation-with-eys-andy-sale/ |access-date=January 30, 2026 |publisher=[[Golden Globe Awards|Golden Globes]]}}{{cite web |author=HFPA |date=September 28, 2022 |title=The HFPA Announces Awards Timetable for the 80th Annual Golden Globe Awards |url=https://goldenglobes.com/articles/the-hfpa-announces-awards-timetable-for-the-80th-annual-golden-globe-awards/ |access-date=January 30, 2026 |publisher=[[Golden Globe Awards|Golden Globes]]}} [[Netflix]] received a total of 36 nominations between film and television, the most of any distributor.{{cite web |last=Goldbart |first=Max |date=December 9, 2024 |title=Netflix Leads the Way on Golden Globes Noms Day |url=https://deadline.com/2024/12/netflix-leads-a24-hbo-golden-globes-nominations-1236198173/ |access-date=December 10, 2024 |website=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |quote=[[Netflix]] leads the pack in the Golden Globes noms for 2024 on both the TV and film side, while [[A24]] and [[HBO]] (& [[HBO Max|Max]]) are well represented. Overall, Netflix picked up 23 TV nominations and 13 film, totaling 36.}} ''Emilia Pérez'' is the first non-English-language film (with some parts of English in the film) to win [[Golden Globe Award for Best Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy|Best Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy]]. ''[[Flow (2024 film)|Flow]]'' is the first [[Latvian language|Latvian]] film to win [[Golden Globe Award for Best Animated Feature Film|Best Animated Feature Film]].{{cite web |title=''Flow'' by Latvian Gints Zilbalodis gets Golden Globe |url=https://eng.lsm.lv/article/culture/culture/06.01.2025-flow-by-latvian-gints-zilbalodis-gets-golden-globe.a582455/ |access-date=January 8, 2025 |publisher=[[Public Broadcasting of Latvia|eng.lsm.lv]]}} [[Fernanda Torres]] became the first [[Brazilian Portuguese|Brazilian]] actress to win a Golden Globe in an acting category,{{cite magazine |last=Rubin |first=Rebecca |date=January 6, 2025 |title=''I'm Still Here'' Star Fernanda Torres Wins Golden Globe 26 Years After Her Mom Was Nominated in Same Category |url=https://variety.com/2025/film/news/fernanda-torres-golden-globes-best-actress-drama-film-1236262309/ |access-date=January 6, 2025 |magazine=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]}} as well as the first Brazilian, first [[South America]]n and first [[Portuguese language|Portuguese-speaking]] actor to win the [[Golden Globe Award for Best Actress in a Motion Picture – Drama]]. She acknowledged her mother [[Fernanda Montenegro]]'s nomination, for ''[[Central Station (film)|Central Station]]'' (1998), in the same category at the [[56th Golden Globe Awards]]. ''Emilia Pérez'' and ''[[Shōgun (2024 TV series)|Shōgun]]'' were the biggest winners of the night, for film and television, respectively, with four wins each.{{cite magazine |date=January 5, 2025 |title=Golden Globes 2025 Winners: ''Emilia Pérez'' Leads with 4 Wins; ''Shōgun'' Tops TV with 4 |magazine=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |last1=Lang |first1=Brent |last2=Moreau |first2=Jordan |url=https://variety.com/2025/film/news/golden-globes-2025-winners-emilia-perez-shogun-1236262416/ |access-date=February 28, 2025 |issn=0042-2738 |oclc=60626328 |quote=...while ''[[The Brutalist]]'', which received the prize for [[Golden Globe Award for Best Motion Picture – Drama|Best Drama]], was close behind with three victories.}} ''[[The Brutalist]]'' was the next biggest film recipient, with three wins;
